text: His count of enchanted objects had diminished by one
comment: The moment Gatsby gets what he wants, it stops being magical. Devastating.

text: the strength of twenty men, and he is brute who has no heart
comment: Van Helsing laying out the threat. The 'no heart' part is key.

text: Till this moment I never knew myself
comment: Elizabeth's moment of self-awareness. The real love story is her learning to see clearly.

text: a single green light, minute and far away, at the end of a dock
comment: This image carries the entire novel. The distance is the point.

text: If you want a picture of the future, imagine a boot stamping on a human face — forever
comment: O'Brien doesn't even pretend there's a utopian goal. Power for power's sake.

text: WAR IS PEACE. FREEDOM IS SLAVERY. IGNORANCE IS STRENGTH.
comment: Three slogans, three impossibilities, yet people believe them. That's the horror.

text: Her voice is full of money
comment: Best character description in all of literature? Maybe.

text: She is tolerable, but not handsome enough to tempt me
comment: The line that launched a thousand enemies-to-lovers stories 😂

text: In vain I have struggled. It will not do. My feelings will not be repressed.
comment: The worst proposal in literary history. He's insulting her while declaring love!

text: How can I describe my emotions at this catastrophe
comment: Victor's horror at his own creation. The birth scene from hell.

text: Freedom is the freedom to say that two plus two make four
comment: The most important sentence Orwell ever wrote.

text: What's in a name? That which we call a rose by any other name would smell as sweet
comment: Shakespeare questioning whether identity is just a label. Still relevant 400 years later.

text: I was benevolent and good; misery made me a fiend
comment: Nature vs nurture in one sentence. The creature wasn't born evil — he was made so.

text: A plague on both your houses
comment: Mercutio blaming both families with his dying breath. The voice of reason dies first.

text: He had one of those rare smiles with a quality of eternal reassurance in it
comment: This is what makes Gatsby so compelling. He makes you feel like you matter.
